subject: Boston Hotels
body:
The W Hotel's best suite available for the night of June 21 is the Marvelous Suite, 580 sq ft, $459 a night.  All one room, king bed.  Amex does not have amenities with the W Hotel.
There is the Mandarin Hotel on Boylston ..they have a premier corner suite, 1080 sq ft, living space and separate bedroom, king bed, one full bath, $1945 per night.  Or the Oriental suite, 1700 sq ft, dining for 6, 1.5 baths, $3950 per night.  Amex DOES have amenities with the Mandarin
http://www.mandarinoriental.com/boston/accommodation/
The Ritz (Amex does have amenities)
http://www.ritzcarlton.com/en/Properties/BostonCommon/Rooms/Default.htm
Four Seasons (Amex does have amenities)
http://www.fourseasons.com/boston/landing_3/?source=gaw10bosS03&kw=four+seasons+boston&creative=30859181809&KW_ID=s5sx2kHJn_dc%7Cpcrid%7C30859181809&gclid=CjkKEQjwttWcBRCuhYjhouveusIBEiQAwjy8IOClhupO-wRV_Pm5SIfVy7sQ6UsY8K91Paj7svsNi5Xw_wcB
The Liberty (more of a boutique hotel)  Signature Suite is $899 per night located downtown.  (Amex does have amenities.)
http://www.libertyhotel.com/accommodations/suites.html
Please advise what hotel you would like me to book for the night of June 21..
